title: Accept and Log Transfusion Requests
description: The system shall accept transfusion requests from healthcare providers, log them in the database with a unique identifier, and store all necessary details.
rationale: To ensure efficient processing and tracking of transfusion requests by blood bank technicians.
business_value: Improves operational efficiency and reduces errors in managing transfusion requests.

source.description: As a blood bank technician, I need accept and log transfusion requests from healthcare providers so that we can process and track transfusion requests efficiently.

acceptance_criteria: The request is accepted and logged in the database with a unique ID.
preconditions: A healthcare provider submits a transfusion request.
postconditions: The system accepts the transfusion request.
The request is stored in the database with all necessary details.
The request has a unique identifier for tracking.
functional_constraints: The system must accurately capture and store all required patient details, blood type, quantity, and urgency.
edge_cases: What if a healthcare provider submits an incomplete request?
How should the system handle duplicate requests for the same patient and blood type?
What if the database is unavailable during a request submission?

interfaces.name: TransfusionRequestAPI
interfaces.description: Interface for receiving transfusion requests from healthcare providers.

interfaces.name: TransfusionRequestDB
interfaces.description: Database where all transfusion requests will be stored with a unique identifier.

test_cases.expected_result: The system should accept the request and log it in the database with a unique ID.

test_cases.description: Submit an incomplete transfusion request (missing patient details).
test_cases.expected_result: The system should reject the request and provide an error message to the healthcare provider.

test_cases.description: Submit a duplicate request for the same patient and blood type.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should accept the request but log it as a duplicate, providing a warning to the healthcare provider.
